target.title: fix(doctor): surface multi-account default routing warnings in preview and lint
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes an issue where `openclaw doctor` preview and `openclaw doctor --lint` did not surface warnings about multi-account channels that lack explicit default routing or complete account-scoped bindings.

## Why This Change Was Made
Surface `collectMissingDefaultAccountBindingWarnings` and `collectMissingExplicitDefaultAccountWarnings` through `collectDoctorPreviewNotes` (preview path) and `healthCheckIds` (lint path). Removed the duplicate direct calls from `doctor-config-flow`. The repair path (`openclaw doctor --fix`) routes the structured check through `runCoreContributionHealthRepair` inside `runGatewayServicesHealth`, matching the pattern used by `runBrowserHealth` for `browser-clawd-profile-residue`. Single source of truth across all three modes: preview, lint, and repair.

## User Impact
Operators running `openclaw doctor` preview, `openclaw doctor --lint`, or `openclaw doctor --fix` now see warnings when multi-account channels lack explicit default routing.

## Evidence

### Preview path (collectDoctorPreviewNotes) — 44 tests
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts --run
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  44 passed (44)
```
Includes the new test: "collects missing default account routing warnings in preview mode" — verifies both "accounts.default is missing" and "no explicit default is set" warnings are surfaced for multi-account configs.

### Lint/repair health check (doctor-core-checks) — 17 tests
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --run
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  17 passed (17)
```
Includes "reports missing default account routing as core health findings" — verifies the structured health check produces `core/doctor/default-account-routing` findings with severity `warning`.

### Repair-mode integration (doctor-health-contributions) — 49 tests
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --run
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  49 passed (49)
```
Includes the new regression test: "routes default-account-routing check through gateway-services repair" — verifies `runDoctorHealthRepairs` is called with `mode: "fix"` and `checks` containing `core/doctor/default-account-routing`.

### Negative control: repair-mode regression caught
Without the `runCoreContributionHealthRepair` call in `runGatewayServicesHealth`, the regression test FAILS:
```
Test Files  1 failed (1) / Tests  1 failed | 48 skipped (49)
FAIL  routes default-account-routing check through gateway-services repair
→ expected runDoctorHealthRepairs to have been called (but it was not)
```
With the fix restored, the test PASSES:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  1 passed | 48 skipped (49)
```
target.author: pick-cat
